raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased a XXXX XXXX from Best Buy for {$1600.00} using my Best Buy Credit Card issued by Citibank. 
I returned that TV in-store on XX/XX/year> and exchanged it for a different model priced at {$1700.00}. 

Best Buy only issued a partial return credit of {$840.00}, plus XXXX unrelated service refunds ( {$380.00} and {$200.00} ). 
These smaller credits do not equal the full {$1600.00} refund that should have been applied for the returned TV. 

As a result, Citibank is still showing XXXX separate promotional balances on my account : {$1400.00} ( the original TV that was returned ) {$1700.00} ( the TV I actually kept ) This means I am being billed for XXXX televisions, even though XXXX of them was returned. 
The remaining refund owed for the returned TV is {$800.00} ( {$1600.00} {$840.00} ). 

Citi has repeatedly closed my prior disputes without addressing this math. They continue to cite partial credits as if they equal the full return amount, which is incorrect. 
This is a clear billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act.
